steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank describes how drivers who believe the toll is already too high will respond to arguments for increasing it. The passage then says advocates should direct arguments toward a more "persuadable" segment — implying these drivers are not persuadable, i.e., they resist or reject such arguments.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — cited in: Means referenced or quoted in those arguments. Drivers opposed to the toll wouldn't be cited in pro-increase arguments. ✗
  • Choice B — apprised of: Means informed or notified. Being informed of arguments says nothing about rejecting them — doesn't capture resistance. ✗
  • Choice C — unsympathetic to: Means not receptive or opposed. Drivers who already think the toll is too high would be unsympathetic to arguments for raising it. ✓
  • Choice D — placated by: Means calmed or appeased by. This implies the arguments would satisfy them — the opposite of the passage's meaning. ✗
C is correct.
